revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「'''芝山はにわ祭'''（しばやまはにわまつり）とは、[[千葉県]][[山武郡]][[芝山町]]で開催される[[祭]]である。 == 概…」
revision.text:
'''芝山はにわ祭'''（しばやまはにわまつり）とは、[[千葉県]][[山武郡]][[芝山町]]で開催される[[祭]]である。
== 概要 == [[成田空港問題]]をめぐり対立する住民を一つにするため、[[1982年]]（昭和57年）に町内有志によって企画された（公式には「古代の素晴らしさを振り返り、思い起こすべく誕生した祭」とされる<ref>{{Cite web|title=第35回芝山はにわ祭が開催されます！|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=芝山町の観光情報をご案内するポータルサイト|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山町観光協会}}</ref>）<ref>{{Cite book|title=写真アルバム 山武の昭和|year=2012|publisher=いき出版|page=130}}</ref>。
以来、毎年11月の第2[[日曜日]]に開催されている<ref>{{Cite web|title=はにわ祭 TOPページ|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=www.haniwakan.com|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}} [[Category:千葉県の祭り]] [[Category:芝山町|はにわまつり]] [[Category:成田空港問題]] {{DEFAULTSORT:しはやまはにわまつり}}

'''芝山はにわ祭'''（しばやまはにわまつり）とは、[[千葉県]][[山武郡]][[芝山町]]で開催される[[祭]]である。
== 概要 == [[成田空港問題]]をめぐり対立する住民を一つにするため、[[1982年]]（昭和57年）に町内有志によって企画された（公式には「古代の素晴らしさを振り返り、思い起こすべく誕生した祭」とされる<ref>{{Cite web|title=第35回芝山はにわ祭が開催されます！|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=芝山町の観光情報をご案内するポータルサイト|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山町観光協会}}</ref>）<ref>{{Cite book|title=写真アルバム 山武の昭和|year=2012|publisher=いき出版|page=130}}</ref>。
以来、毎年11月の第2[[日曜日]]に開催されている<ref>{{Cite web|title=はにわ祭 TOPページ|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=www.haniwakan.com|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}</ref>。
地元の小中学生らが古代人に扮して儀式やパレードを行い、最後に古代人が現代人へのメッセージを残して「昇天」する<ref>{{Cite web|title=まつり・イベント|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=芝山町の観光情報をご案内するポータルサイト|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山町観光協会}}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}} [[Category:千葉県の祭り]] [[Category:芝山町|はにわまつり]] [[Category:成田空港問題]] {{DEFAULTSORT:しはやまはにわまつり}}

'''芝山はにわ祭'''（しばやまはにわまつり）とは、[[千葉県]][[山武郡]][[芝山町]]で開催される[[祭]]である。
== 概要 == [[成田空港問題]]をめぐり対立する住民を一つにするため、[[1982年]]（昭和57年）に町内有志によって企画された（公式には「古代の素晴らしさを振り返り、思い起こすべく誕生した祭」とされる<ref>{{Cite web|title=第35回芝山はにわ祭が開催されます！|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=芝山町の観光情報をご案内するポータルサイト|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山町観光協会}}</ref>）<ref>{{Cite book|title=写真アルバム 山武の昭和|year=2012|publisher=いき出版|page=130}}</ref>。祭は町内の融和に貢献したとされ、現在は[[地域おこし|町おこし]]のためのイベントとして位置づけられている<ref>{{Cite web|title=「古墳の里」でタイムスリップ 芝山町|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=日本経済新聞 電子版|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>。
毎年11月の第2[[日曜日]]に開催されている<ref>{{Cite web|title=はにわ祭 TOPページ|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=www.haniwakan.com|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}</ref>。
地元の小中学生らが古代人に扮して儀式やパレードを行い、最後に古代人が現代人へのメッセージを残して「昇天」する<ref>{{Cite web|title=まつり・イベント|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=芝山町の観光情報をご案内するポータルサイト|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山町観光協会}}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}} [[Category:千葉県の祭り]] [[Category:芝山町|はにわまつり]] [[Category:成田空港問題]] {{DEFAULTSORT:しはやまはにわまつり}}

'''芝山はにわ祭'''（しばやまはにわまつり）とは、[[千葉県]][[山武郡]][[芝山町]]で開催される[[祭]]である。
== 概要 == [[成田空港問題]]をめぐり対立する住民を一つにするため、[[1982年]]（昭和57年）に町内有志によって企画された（公式には「古代の素晴らしさを振り返り、思い起こすべく誕生した祭」とされる<ref>{{Cite web|title=第35回芝山はにわ祭が開催されます！|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=芝山町の観光情報をご案内するポータルサイト|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山町観光協会}}</ref>）<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=わが街の学芸員：／５止 芝山古墳・はにわ博物館 かつて５００基超す古墳 ／千葉|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170807/ddl/k12/040/136000c|website=毎日新聞|accessdate=2019-03-09|date=2017-08-07}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|title=写真アルバム 山武の昭和|year=2012|publisher=いき出版|page=130}}</ref>。祭は町内の融和に貢献したとされ、現在は[[地域おこし|町おこし]]のためのイベントとして位置づけられている<ref>{{Cite web|title=「古墳の里」でタイムスリップ 芝山町|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=日本経済新聞 電子版|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>。
毎年11月の第2[[日曜日]]に開催されている<ref>{{Cite web|title=はにわ祭 TOPページ|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=www.haniwakan.com|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}</ref>。
祭の趣旨は、現代に降臨した「古代人」と交流し、芝山を人に優しい、住みやすい町にすることを古代人と約束するというものである。小中学生ら町民が古代人に扮して儀式やパレードを行い、最後に古代人が現代人へのメッセージを残し、「昇天」する<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=まつり・イベント|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=芝山町の観光情報をご案内するポータルサイト|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山町観光協会}}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}} [[Category:千葉県の祭り]] [[Category:芝山町|はにわまつり]] [[Category:成田空港問題]] {{DEFAULTSORT:しはやまはにわまつり}}

毎年11月の第2[[日曜日]]に開催されている<ref>{{Cite web|title=はにわ祭 TOPページ|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=www.haniwakan.com|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}</ref>。

== 概要 == [[成田空港問題]]をめぐり対立する住民を一つにするため、[[1982年]]（昭和57年）に町内有志によって企画された（公式には「古代の素晴らしさを振り返り、思い起こすべく誕生した祭」とされる<ref>{{Cite web|title=第35回芝山はにわ祭が開催されます！|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=芝山町の観光情報をご案内するポータルサイト|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山町観光協会}}</ref>）<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=わが街の学芸員：／５止 芝山古墳・はにわ博物館 かつて５００基超す古墳 ／千葉|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170807/ddl/k12/040/136000c|website=毎日新聞|accessdate=2019-03-09|date=2017-08-07}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|title=写真アルバム 山武の昭和|year=2012|publisher=いき出版|page=130}}</ref>。祭は町内の融和に貢献したとされ、現在は[[地域おこし|町おこし]]のためのイベントとして位置づけられている<ref>{{Cite web|title=「古墳の里」でタイムスリップ 芝山町|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=日本経済新聞 電子版|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>。

祭は芝山町名物の古墳群や埴輪に因んでおり、現代に降臨した「古代人」と交流し、芝山を人に優しい、住みやすい町にすることを古代人と約束するというあらましである。の趣旨小中学生ら町民が古代人に扮して儀式やパレードを行い、最後に古代人が現代人へのメッセージを残し、「昇天」する<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=まつり・イベント|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=芝山町の観光情報をご案内するポータルサイト|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山町観光協会}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=古代にタイムスリップ ＡＫＢの永尾さんも登場 芝山はにわ祭|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=www.chibanippo.co.jp|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=千葉日報オンライン|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>。

== 脚注 == {{Reflist}} [[Category:千葉県の祭り]] [[Category:芝山町|はにわまつり]] [[Category:成田空港問題]] {{DEFAULTSORT:しはやまはにわまつり}} [[Category:11月]]

'''芝山はにわ祭'''（しばやまはにわまつり）とは、[[千葉県]][[山武郡]][[芝山町]]で開催される[[祭]]である。
== 概要 == [[成田空港問題]]をめぐり対立する住民を一つにするため町内有志によって企画され（公式には「古代の素晴らしさを振り返り、思い起こすべく誕生した祭」とされる<ref>{{Cite web|title=第35回芝山はにわ祭が開催されます！|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=芝山町の観光情報をご案内するポータルサイト|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山町観光協会}}</ref>）、[[1982年]]（昭和57年）[[10月31日]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2489/2015ShibayamaYouran_history_p8-15.pdf|title=芝山町60年の変遷|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山町|format=PDF}}</ref>に初めて開催、町内の融和に貢献した<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=わが街の学芸員：／５止 芝山古墳・はにわ博物館 かつて５００基超す古墳 ／千葉|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170807/ddl/k12/040/136000c|website=毎日新聞|accessdate=2019-03-09|date=2017-08-07}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|title=写真アルバム 山武の昭和|year=2012|publisher=いき出版|page=130}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=「古墳の里」でタイムスリップ 芝山町|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=日本経済新聞 電子版|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>。
現在は[[地域おこし|町おこし]]のためのイベントとして位置づけられており、毎年11月の第2[[日曜日]]に開催されている<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=はにわ祭 TOPページ|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=www.haniwakan.com|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}</ref>。
祭は芝山町名物の[[古墳群]]や[[埴輪]]に因んでおり、現代に降臨した「古代人」と交流し、芝山を人に優しい、住みやすい町にすることを古代人と約束するというあらましである。祭では小中学生ら町民が古代人に扮して儀式やパレードを行い、最後に古代人が現代人へのメッセージを残し、「昇天」する<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=まつり・イベント|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=芝山町の観光情報をご案内するポータルサイト|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山町観光協会}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=古代にタイムスリップ ＡＫＢの永尾さんも登場 芝山はにわ祭|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=www.chibanippo.co.jp|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=千葉日報オンライン|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>。

revision.comment: Bot作業依頼#Cite webの和書引数追加
revision.text:
'''芝山はにわ祭'''（しばやまはにわまつり）とは、[[千葉県]][[山武郡]][[芝山町]]で開催される[[祭]]である。
== 概要 == [[成田空港問題]]をめぐり対立する住民を一つにするため町内有志によって企画され<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=わが街の学芸員：／５止 芝山古墳・はにわ博物館 かつて５００基超す古墳 ／千葉|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170807/ddl/k12/040/136000c|website=毎日新聞|accessdate=2019-03-09|date=2017-08-07}}</ref>（公式には「古代の素晴らしさを振り返り、思い起こすべく誕生した祭」とされる<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=第35回芝山はにわ祭が開催されます！|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=芝山町の観光情報をご案内するポータルサイト|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山町観光協会}}</ref>）、[[1982年]]（昭和57年）[[10月31日]]<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2489/2015ShibayamaYouran_history_p8-15.pdf|title=芝山町60年の変遷|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山町|format=PDF}}</ref>に初めて開催、町内の融和に貢献した<ref>{{Cite book|title=写真アルバム 山武の昭和|year=2012|publisher=いき出版|page=130}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|和書|title=「古墳の里」でタイムスリップ 芝山町|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=日本経済新聞 電子版|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>。
現在は[[地域おこし|町おこし]]のためのイベントとして位置づけられており、毎年11月の第2[[日曜日]]に開催されている<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=はにわ祭 TOPページ|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=www.haniwakan.com|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}</ref>。
祭は芝山町名物の[[古墳群]]や[[埴輪]]に因んでおり、現代に降臨した「古代人」と交流し、芝山を人に優しい、住みやすい町にすることを古代人と約束するというあらましである。祭では小中学生ら町民が古代人に扮して儀式やパレードを行い、最後に古代人が現代人へのメッセージを残し、「昇天」する<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=まつり・イベント|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=芝山町の観光情報をご案内するポータルサイト|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山町観光協会}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=古代にタイムスリップ ＡＫＢの永尾さんも登場 芝山はにわ祭|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=www.chibanippo.co.jp|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=千葉日報オンライン|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[芝山古墳・はにわ博物館]] * [[芝山古墳群]]・[[山田・宝馬古墳群]]・[[高田二号墳]] * [[芝山はにわ道]] * [[村山元英]] * [[土井脩司]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/0000004151.html 芝山町ホームページ - 観光・イベント - 芝山はにわ祭] * 論文：[https://ci.nii.ac.jp/naid/110009856963 遺跡まつりと地域アイデンティティ : 「芝山はにわ祭」の事例分析から]
[[Category:千葉県の祭り]] [[Category:芝山町|はにわまつり]] [[Category:成田空港問題]] {{DEFAULTSORT:しはやまはにわまつり}} [[Category:11月]] [[Category:埴輪]]

revision.text:
'''Shibayama Haniwa Festival''' (芝山はにわ祭) is a festival held in {{ill|Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}.
== Overview == The Shibayama Haniwa Festival was initially conceived by local volunteers as a way to unite residents divided by the {{ill|Narita Airport dispute|ja|成田空港問題}}.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Curator in My Town: Shibayama Ancient Tomb and Haniwa Museum, Over 500 Kofun Once Existed / Chiba|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170807/ddl/k12/040/136000c|website=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-03-09|date=2017-08-07}}</ref> Officially described as a festival "born to reflect on and recall the wonders of ancient times,"<ref>{{Cite web|title=The 35th Shibayama Haniwa Festival is being held!|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref> it was first held on October 31, 1982 (Showa 57)<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2489/2015ShibayamaYouran_history_p8-15.pdf|title=Sixty Years of Shibayama History|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town|format=PDF}}</ref> and contributed significantly to fostering harmony among local residents.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Photo Album: The Showa Era in Sanbu|year=2012|publisher=Iki Publishing|page=130}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title="Slip Back in Time in the 'Land of Ancient Tombs': Shibayama Town"|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=Nikkei Electronic Edition|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>
Today, it is positioned as a community revitalization event, held annually on the second Sunday of November.<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Haniwa Festival Main Page|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=Haniwa Museum Website|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Ancient Tomb and Haniwa Museum}}</ref>
The festival draws inspiration from Shibayama's iconic {{ill|ancient burial mounds|ja|古墳群}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. The central concept involves interaction with "ancient people" who have descended into modern times to make a promise with the people of Shibayama to create a kind and livable town. During the festival, local residents, including elementary and middle school students, dress as ancient people to perform rituals and parades. The event concludes with the ancient people delivering messages to modern attendees before "ascending to the heavens."<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals and Events|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Travel Back in Time to Ancient Japan, Featuring AKB's Nagao-san: Shibayama Haniwa Festival|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=Chiba Nippo Online|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Chiba Nippo Online|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Shibayama Ancient Tomb and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}}, {{ill|Yamada-Houma Kofun Cluster|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群}}, and {{ill|Takada No. 2 Kofun|ja|高田二号墳}} * {{ill|Shibayama Haniwa Road|ja|芝山はにわ道}} * {{ill|Motohide Murayama|ja|村山元英}} * {{ill|Shuji Doi|ja|土井脩司}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/0000004151.html Shibayama Town Official Website - Tourism & Events - Shibayama Haniwa Festival] * [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1050001338458012928 Research Paper: "Festival and Regional Identity: Case Study Analysis of the Shibayama Haniwa Festival"]
[[Category:Festivals in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shibayama]] [[Category:Narita Airport Dispute]] [[Category:November]] [[Category:Haniwa]] [[ja:芝山はにわ祭]]

'''Shibayama Haniwa Festival''' (芝山はにわ祭) is a festival held in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|lt=Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}.
== Overview == The Shibayama Haniwa Festival was initially conceived by local volunteers as a way to unite residents divided by the {{ill|Narita Airport dispute|ja|成田空港問題}}.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Curator in My Town: Shibayama Ancient Tomb and Haniwa Museum, Over 500 Kofun Once Existed / Chiba|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170807/ddl/k12/040/136000c|website=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-03-09|date=2017-08-07}}</ref> Officially described as a festival "born to reflect on and recall the wonders of ancient times,"<ref>{{Cite web|title=The 35th Shibayama Haniwa Festival is being held!|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref> it was first held on October 31, 1982 (Showa 57)<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2489/2015ShibayamaYouran_history_p8-15.pdf|title=Sixty Years of Shibayama History|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town|format=PDF}}</ref> and contributed significantly to fostering harmony among local residents.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Photo Album: The Showa Era in Sanbu|year=2012|publisher=Iki Publishing|page=130}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title="Slip Back in Time in the 'Land of Ancient Tombs': Shibayama Town"|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=Nikkei Electronic Edition|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>
Today, it is positioned as a community revitalization event, held annually on the second Sunday of November.<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Haniwa Festival Main Page|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=Haniwa Museum Website|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Ancient Tomb and Haniwa Museum}}</ref>
The festival draws inspiration from Shibayama's iconic {{ill|ancient burial mounds|ja|古墳群}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. The central concept involves interaction with "ancient people" who have descended into modern times to make a promise with the people of Shibayama to create a kind and livable town. During the festival, local residents, including elementary and middle school students, dress as ancient people to perform rituals and parades. The event concludes with the ancient people delivering messages to modern attendees before "ascending to the heavens."<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals and Events|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Travel Back in Time to Ancient Japan, Featuring AKB's Nagao-san: Shibayama Haniwa Festival|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=Chiba Nippo Online|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Chiba Nippo Online|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Shibayama Ancient Tomb and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}}, {{ill|Yamada-Houma Kofun Cluster|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群}}, and {{ill|Takada No. 2 Kofun|ja|高田二号墳}} * {{ill|Shibayama Haniwa Road|ja|芝山はにわ道}} * {{ill|Motohide Murayama|ja|村山元英}} * {{ill|Shuji Doi|ja|土井脩司}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/0000004151.html Shibayama Town Official Website - Tourism & Events - Shibayama Haniwa Festival] * [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1050001338458012928 Research Paper: "Festival and Regional Identity: Case Study Analysis of the Shibayama Haniwa Festival"]

revision.comment: Bumping for adding the Kofun template
revision.text:
'''Shibayama Haniwa Festival''' (芝山はにわ祭) is a festival held in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|lt=Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}.
== Overview == The Shibayama Haniwa Festival was initially conceived by local volunteers as a way to unite residents divided by the {{ill|Narita Airport dispute|ja|成田空港問題}}.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Curator in My Town: Shibayama Ancient Tomb and Haniwa Museum, Over 500 Kofun Once Existed / Chiba|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170807/ddl/k12/040/136000c|website=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-03-09|date=2017-08-07}}</ref> Officially described as a festival "born to reflect on and recall the wonders of ancient times,"<ref>{{Cite web|title=The 35th Shibayama Haniwa Festival is being held!|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref> it was first held on October 31, 1982 (Showa 57)<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2489/2015ShibayamaYouran_history_p8-15.pdf|title=Sixty Years of Shibayama History|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town|format=PDF}}</ref> and contributed significantly to fostering harmony among local residents.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Photo Album: The Showa Era in Sanbu|year=2012|publisher=Iki Publishing|page=130}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title="Slip Back in Time in the 'Land of Ancient Tombs': Shibayama Town"|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=Nikkei Electronic Edition|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>
Today, it is positioned as a community revitalization event, held annually on the second Sunday of November.<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Haniwa Festival Main Page|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=Haniwa Museum Website|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Ancient Tomb and Haniwa Museum}}</ref>
The festival draws inspiration from Shibayama's iconic {{ill|ancient burial mounds|ja|古墳群}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. The central concept involves interaction with "ancient people" who have descended into modern times to make a promise with the people of Shibayama to create a kind and livable town. During the festival, local residents, including elementary and middle school students, dress as ancient people to perform rituals and parades. The event concludes with the ancient people delivering messages to modern attendees before "ascending to the heavens."<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals and Events|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Travel Back in Time to Ancient Japan, Featuring AKB's Nagao-san: Shibayama Haniwa Festival|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=Chiba Nippo Online|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Chiba Nippo Online|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>

The event concludes with the ancient people delivering messages to modern attendees before "ascending to the heavens."<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals and Events|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Travel Back in Time to Ancient Japan, Featuring AKB's Nagao-san: Shibayama Haniwa Festival|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=Chiba Nippo Online|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Chiba Nippo Online|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>

'''Shibayama Haniwa Festival''' (芝山はにわ祭) is a festival held in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|lt=Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}.
== Overview == The Shibayama Haniwa Festival was initially conceived by local volunteers as a way to unite residents divided by the {{ill|Narita Airport dispute|ja|成田空港問題}}.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Curator in My Town: Shibayama Ancient Tomb and Haniwa Museum, Over 500 Kofun Once Existed / Chiba|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170807/ddl/k12/040/136000c|website=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-03-09|date=2017-08-07}}</ref> Officially described as a festival "born to reflect on and recall the wonders of ancient times,"<ref>{{Cite web|title=The 35th Shibayama Haniwa Festival is being held!|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref> it was first held on October 31, 1982 (Showa 57)<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2489/2015ShibayamaYouran_history_p8-15.pdf|title=Sixty Years of Shibayama History|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town|format=PDF}}</ref> and contributed significantly to fostering harmony among local residents.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Photo Album: The Showa Era in Sanbu|year=2012|publisher=Iki Publishing|page=130}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title="Slip Back in Time in the 'Land of Ancient Tombs': Shibayama Town"|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=Nikkei Electronic Edition|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>
Today, it is positioned as a community revitalization event, held annually on the second Sunday of November.<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Haniwa Festival Main Page|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=Haniwa Museum Website|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Ancient Tomb and Haniwa Museum}}</ref>
The festival draws inspiration from Shibayama's iconic {{ill|ancient burial mounds|ja|古墳群}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. The central concept involves interaction with "ancient people" who have descended into modern times to make a promise with the people of Shibayama to create a kind and livable town. During the festival, local residents, including elementary and middle school students, dress as ancient people to perform rituals and parades. The event concludes with the ancient people delivering messages to modern attendees before "ascending to the heavens."<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals and Events|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Travel Back in Time to Ancient Japan, Featuring AKB's Nagao-san: Shibayama Haniwa Festival|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=Chiba Nippo Online|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Chiba Nippo Online|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>

== Overview == The Shibayama Haniwa Festival was initially conceived by local volunteers as a way to unite residents divided by the {{ill|Narita Airport dispute|ja|成田空港問題}}.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Curator in My Town: Shibayama Ancient Tomb and Haniwa Museum, Over 500 Kofun Once Existed / Chiba|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170807/ddl/k12/040/136000c|website=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-03-09|date=2017-08-07}}</ref> Officially described as a festival "born to reflect on and recall the wonders of ancient times,"<ref>{{Cite web|title=The 35th Shibayama Haniwa Festival is being held!|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref> it was first held on October 31, 1982 (Showa 57)<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2489/2015ShibayamaYouran_history_p8-15.pdf|title=Sixty Years of Shibayama History|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town|format=PDF}}</ref> and contributed significantly to fostering harmony among local residents.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Photo Album: The Showa Era in Sanbu|year=2012|publisher=Iki Publishing|page=130}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title="Slip Back in Time in the 'Land of Ancient Tombs': Shibayama Town"|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=Nikkei Electronic Edition|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>

The festival draws inspiration from Shibayama's iconic {{ill|ancient burial mounds|ja|古墳群}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. The central concept involves interaction with "ancient people" who have descended into modern times to make a promise with the people of Shibayama to create a kind and livable town. During the festival, local residents, including elementary and middle school students, dress as ancient people to perform rituals and parades. The event concludes with the ancient people delivering messages to modern attendees before "ascending to the heavens."<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals and Events|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Travel Back in Time to Ancient Japan, Featuring AKB's Nagao-san: Shibayama Haniwa Festival|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=Chiba Nippo Online|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Chiba Nippo Online|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>

'''Shibayama Haniwa Festival''' (芝山はにわ祭) is a festival held in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|lt=Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}.
== Overview == The Shibayama Haniwa Festival was initially conceived by local volunteers as a way to unite residents divided by the {{ill|Narita Airport dispute|ja|成田空港問題}}.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Curator in My Town: Shibayama Ancient Tomb and Haniwa Museum, Over 500 Kofun Once Existed / Chiba|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170807/ddl/k12/040/136000c|website=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-03-09|date=2017-08-07}}</ref> Officially described as a festival "born to reflect on and recall the wonders of ancient times,"<ref>{{Cite web|title=The 35th Shibayama Haniwa Festival is being held!|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref> it was first held on October 31, 1982 (Showa 57)<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2489/2015ShibayamaYouran_history_p8-15.pdf|title=Sixty Years of Shibayama History|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town|format=PDF}}</ref> and contributed significantly to fostering harmony among local residents.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Photo Album: The Showa Era in Sanbu|year=2012|publisher=Iki Publishing|page=130}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title="Slip Back in Time in the 'Land of Ancient Tombs': Shibayama Town"|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=Nikkei Electronic Edition|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>
Today, it is positioned as a community revitalization event, held annually on the second Sunday of November.<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Haniwa Festival Main Page|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=Haniwa Museum Website|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Ancient Tomb and Haniwa Museum}}</ref>
The festival draws inspiration from Shibayama's iconic {{ill|ancient burial mounds|ja|古墳群}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. The central concept involves interaction with "ancient people" who have descended into modern times to make a promise with the people of Shibayama to create a kind and livable town. During the festival, local residents, including elementary and middle school students, dress as ancient people to perform rituals and parades. The event concludes with the ancient people delivering messages to modern attendees before "ascending to the heavens."<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals and Events|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Travel Back in Time to Ancient Japan, Featuring AKB's Nagao-san: Shibayama Haniwa Festival|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=Chiba Nippo Online|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Chiba Nippo Online|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>

'''芝山はにわ祭'''（しばやまはにわまつり）とは、[[千葉県]][[山武郡]][[芝山町]]で開催される[[祭]]である。
== 概要 == [[成田空港問題]]をめぐり対立する住民を一つにするため町内有志によって企画され<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=わが街の学芸員：／５止 芝山古墳・はにわ博物館 かつて５００基超す古墳 ／千葉|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170807/ddl/k12/040/136000c|website=毎日新聞|accessdate=2019-03-09|date=2017-08-07}}</ref>（公式には「古代の素晴らしさを振り返り、思い起こすべく誕生した祭」とされる<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=第35回芝山はにわ祭が開催されます！|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=芝山町の観光情報をご案内するポータルサイト|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山町観光協会}}</ref>）、[[1982年]]（昭和57年）[[10月31日]]<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2489/2015ShibayamaYouran_history_p8-15.pdf|title=芝山町60年の変遷|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山町|format=PDF}}</ref>に初めて開催、町内の融和に貢献した<ref>{{Cite book|title=写真アルバム 山武の昭和|year=2012|publisher=いき出版|page=130}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|和書|title=「古墳の里」でタイムスリップ 芝山町|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=日本経済新聞 電子版|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>。
現在は[[地域おこし|町おこし]]のためのイベントとして位置づけられており、毎年11月の第2[[日曜日]]に開催されている<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=はにわ祭 TOPページ|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=www.haniwakan.com|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}}</ref>。
祭は芝山町名物の[[古墳群]]や[[埴輪]]に因んでおり、現代に降臨した「古代人」と交流し、芝山を人に優しい、住みやすい町にすることを古代人と約束するというあらましである。祭では小中学生ら町民が古代人に扮して儀式やパレードを行い、最後に古代人が現代人へのメッセージを残し、「昇天」する<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=まつり・イベント|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=芝山町の観光情報をご案内するポータルサイト|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=芝山町観光協会}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=古代にタイムスリップ ＡＫＢの永尾さんも登場 芝山はにわ祭|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=www.chibanippo.co.jp|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=千葉日報オンライン|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[芝山古墳・はにわ博物館]] * [[芝山古墳群]]・[[山田・宝馬古墳群]]・[[高田二号墳]] * [[芝山はにわ道]] * [[村山元英]] * [[土井脩司]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/0000004151.html 芝山町ホームページ - 観光・イベント - 芝山はにわ祭] * 論文：[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1050001338458012928 遺跡まつりと地域アイデンティティ : 「芝山はにわ祭」の事例分析から]
{{成田空港問題}} [[Category:千葉県の祭り]] [[Category:芝山町|はにわまつり]] [[Category:成田空港問題]] {{DEFAULTSORT:しはやまはにわまつり}} [[Category:11月]] [[Category:埴輪]]

revision.text:
'''Shibayama Haniwa Festival''' (芝山はにわ祭) is a festival held in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|lt=Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}.
== Overview == The Shibayama Haniwa Festival was initially conceived by local volunteers as a way to unite residents divided by the {{ill|Narita Airport dispute|ja|成田空港問題}}.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Curator in My Town: Shibayama Ancient Tomb and Haniwa Museum, Over 500 Kofun Once Existed / Chiba|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170807/ddl/k12/040/136000c|website=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-03-09|date=2017-08-07}}</ref> Officially described as a festival "born to reflect on and recall the wonders of ancient times,"<ref>{{Cite web|title=The 35th Shibayama Haniwa Festival is being held!|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref> it was first held on October 31, 1982 (Showa 57)<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2489/2015ShibayamaYouran_history_p8-15.pdf|title=Sixty Years of Shibayama History|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town|format=PDF}}</ref> and contributed significantly to fostering harmony among local residents.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Photo Album: The Showa Era in Sanbu|year=2012|publisher=Iki Publishing|page=130}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title="Slip Back in Time in the 'Land of Ancient Tombs': Shibayama Town"|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=Nikkei Electronic Edition|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>
Today, it is positioned as a community revitalization event, held annually on the second Sunday of November.<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Haniwa Festival Main Page|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=Haniwa Museum Website|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Ancient Tomb and Haniwa Museum}}</ref>
The festival draws inspiration from Shibayama's iconic {{ill|ancient burial mounds|ja|古墳群}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. The central concept involves interaction with "ancient people" who have descended into modern times to make a promise with the people of Shibayama to create a kind and livable town. During the festival, local residents, including elementary and middle school students, dress as ancient people to perform rituals and parades. The event concludes with the ancient people delivering messages to modern attendees before "ascending to the heavens."<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals and Events|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Travel Back in Time to Ancient Japan, Featuring AKB's Nagao-san: Shibayama Haniwa Festival|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=Chiba Nippo Online|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Chiba Nippo Online|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Shibayama Ancient Tomb and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}}, {{ill|Yamada-Houma Kofun Cluster|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群}}, and {{ill|Takada No. 2 Kofun|ja|高田二号墳}} * {{ill|Shibayama Haniwa Road|ja|芝山はにわ道}} * {{ill|Motohide Murayama|ja|村山元英}} * {{ill|Shuji Doi|ja|土井脩司}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/0000004151.html Shibayama Town Official Website - Tourism & Events - Shibayama Haniwa Festival] * [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1050001338458012928 Research Paper: "Festival and Regional Identity: Case Study Analysis of the Shibayama Haniwa Festival"]

Today, it is positioned as a community revitalization event, held annually on the second Sunday of November.<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Haniwa Festival Main Page|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=Haniwa Museum Website|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Ancient Tomb and Haniwa Museum}}</ref>

During the festival, local residents, including elementary and middle school students, dress as ancient people to perform rituals and parades. The event concludes with the ancient people delivering messages to modern attendees before "ascending to the heavens."<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals and Events|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Travel Back in Time to Ancient Japan, Featuring AKB's Nagao-san: Shibayama Haniwa Festival|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=Chiba Nippo Online|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Chiba Nippo Online|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>

'''Shibayama Haniwa Festival''' (芝山はにわ祭) is a festival held in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|lt=Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}.
== Overview == The Shibayama Haniwa Festival was initially conceived by local volunteers as a way to unite residents divided by the {{ill|Narita Airport dispute|ja|成田空港問題}}.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Curator in My Town: Shibayama Ancient Tomb and Haniwa Museum, Over 500 Kofun Once Existed / Chiba|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170807/ddl/k12/040/136000c|website=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-03-09|date=2017-08-07}}</ref> Officially described as a festival "born to reflect on and recall the wonders of ancient times,"<ref>{{Cite web|title=The 35th Shibayama Haniwa Festival is being held!|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref> it was first held on October 31, 1982 (Showa 57)<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2489/2015ShibayamaYouran_history_p8-15.pdf|title=Sixty Years of Shibayama History|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town|format=PDF}}</ref> and contributed significantly to fostering harmony among local residents.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Photo Album: The Showa Era in Sanbu|year=2012|publisher=Iki Publishing|page=130}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title="Slip Back in Time in the 'Land of Ancient Tombs': Shibayama Town"|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=Nikkei Electronic Edition|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>
Today, it is positioned as a community revitalization event, held annually on the second Sunday of November.<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Haniwa Festival Main Page|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=Haniwa Museum Website|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Ancient Tomb and Haniwa Museum}}</ref>
The festival draws inspiration from Shibayama's iconic {{ill|ancient burial mounds|ja|古墳群}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. The central concept involves interaction with "ancient people" who have descended into modern times to make a promise with the people of Shibayama to create a kind and livable town. During the festival, local residents, including elementary and middle school students, dress as ancient people to perform rituals and parades. The event concludes with the ancient people delivering messages to modern attendees before "ascending to the heavens."<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals and Events|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Travel Back in Time to Ancient Japan, Featuring AKB's Nagao-san: Shibayama Haniwa Festival|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=Chiba Nippo Online|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Chiba Nippo Online|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Shibayama Ancient Tomb and Haniwa Museum" to "Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum"
revision.text:
'''Shibayama Haniwa Festival''' (芝山はにわ祭) is a festival held in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|lt=Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}.
== Overview == The Shibayama Haniwa Festival was initially conceived by local volunteers as a way to unite residents divided by the {{ill|Narita Airport dispute|ja|成田空港問題}}.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Curator in My Town: Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum, Over 500 Kofun Once Existed / Chiba|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170807/ddl/k12/040/136000c|website=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-03-09|date=2017-08-07}}</ref> Officially described as a festival "born to reflect on and recall the wonders of ancient times,"<ref>{{Cite web|title=The 35th Shibayama Haniwa Festival is being held!|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref> it was first held on October 31, 1982 (Showa 57)<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2489/2015ShibayamaYouran_history_p8-15.pdf|title=Sixty Years of Shibayama History|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town|format=PDF}}</ref> and contributed significantly to fostering harmony among local residents.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Photo Album: The Showa Era in Sanbu|year=2012|publisher=Iki Publishing|page=130}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title="Slip Back in Time in the 'Land of Ancient Tombs': Shibayama Town"|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=Nikkei Electronic Edition|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>
Today, it is positioned as a community revitalization event, held annually on the second Sunday of November.<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Haniwa Festival Main Page|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=Haniwa Museum Website|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum}}</ref>
The festival draws inspiration from Shibayama's iconic {{ill|ancient burial mounds|ja|古墳群}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. The central concept involves interaction with "ancient people" who have descended into modern times to make a promise with the people of Shibayama to create a kind and livable town. During the festival, local residents, including elementary and middle school students, dress as ancient people to perform rituals and parades. The event concludes with the ancient people delivering messages to modern attendees before "ascending to the heavens."<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals and Events|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Travel Back in Time to Ancient Japan, Featuring AKB's Nagao-san: Shibayama Haniwa Festival|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=Chiba Nippo Online|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Chiba Nippo Online|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}}, {{ill|Yamada-Houma Kofun Cluster|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群}}, and {{ill|Takada No. 2 Kofun|ja|高田二号墳}} * {{ill|Shibayama Haniwa Road|ja|芝山はにわ道}} * {{ill|Motohide Murayama|ja|村山元英}} * {{ill|Shuji Doi|ja|土井脩司}}

'''Shibayama Haniwa Festival''' (芝山はにわ祭) is a festival held in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|lt=Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}.
== Overview == The Shibayama Haniwa Festival was initially conceived by local volunteers as a way to unite residents divided by the {{ill|Narita Airport dispute|ja|成田空港問題}}.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Curator in My Town: Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum, Over 500 Kofun Once Existed / Chiba|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170807/ddl/k12/040/136000c|website=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-03-09|date=2017-08-07}}</ref> Officially described as a festival "born to reflect on and recall the wonders of ancient times,"<ref>{{Cite web|title=The 35th Shibayama Haniwa Festival is being held!|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref> it was first held on October 31, 1982 (Showa 57)<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2489/2015ShibayamaYouran_history_p8-15.pdf|title=Sixty Years of Shibayama History|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town|format=PDF}}</ref> and contributed significantly to fostering harmony among local residents.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Photo Album: The Showa Era in Sanbu|year=2012|publisher=Iki Publishing|page=130}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title="Slip Back in Time in the 'Land of Ancient Tombs': Shibayama Town"|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=Nikkei Electronic Edition|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>
Today, it is positioned as a community revitalization event, held annually on the second Sunday of November.<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Haniwa Festival Main Page|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=Haniwa Museum Website|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum}}</ref>
The festival draws inspiration from Shibayama's iconic {{ill|ancient burial mounds|ja|古墳群}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. The central concept involves interaction with "ancient people" who have descended into modern times to make a promise with the people of Shibayama to create a kind and livable town. During the festival, local residents, including elementary and middle school students, dress as ancient people to perform rituals and parades. The event concludes with the ancient people delivering messages to modern attendees before "ascending to the heavens."<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals and Events|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Travel Back in Time to Ancient Japan, Featuring AKB's Nagao-san: Shibayama Haniwa Festival|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=Chiba Nippo Online|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Chiba Nippo Online|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>

The festival draws inspiration from Shibayama's iconic {{ill|ancient burial mounds|ja|古墳群}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. The central concept involves interaction with "ancient people" who have descended into modern times to make a promise with the people of Shibayama to create a kind and livable town. During the festival, local residents, including elementary and middle school students, dress as ancient people to perform rituals and parades. The event concludes with the ancient people delivering messages to modern attendees before "ascending to the heavens."<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals and Events|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Travel Back in Time to Ancient Japan, Featuring AKB's Nagao-san: Shibayama Haniwa Festival|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=Chiba Nippo Online|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Chiba Nippo Online|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>

'''Shibayama Haniwa Festival''' (芝山はにわ祭) is a festival held in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|lt=Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}.
== Overview == The Shibayama Haniwa Festival was initially conceived by local volunteers as a way to unite residents divided by the {{ill|Narita Airport dispute|ja|成田空港問題}}.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Curator in My Town: Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum, Over 500 Kofun Once Existed / Chiba|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170807/ddl/k12/040/136000c|website=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-03-09|date=2017-08-07}}</ref> Officially described as a festival "born to reflect on and recall the wonders of ancient times,"<ref>{{Cite web|title=The 35th Shibayama Haniwa Festival is being held!|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref> it was first held on October 31, 1982 (Showa 57)<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2489/2015ShibayamaYouran_history_p8-15.pdf|title=Sixty Years of Shibayama History|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town|format=PDF}}</ref> and contributed significantly to fostering harmony among local residents.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Photo Album: The Showa Era in Sanbu|year=2012|publisher=Iki Publishing|page=130}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title="Slip Back in Time in the 'Land of Ancient Tombs': Shibayama Town"|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=Nikkei Electronic Edition|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>
Today, it is positioned as a community revitalization event, held annually on the second Sunday of November.<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Haniwa Festival Main Page|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=Haniwa Museum Website|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum}}</ref>
The festival draws inspiration from Shibayama's iconic {{ill|ancient burial mounds|ja|古墳群}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. The central concept involves interaction with "ancient people" who have descended into modern times to make a promise with the people of Shibayama to create a kind and livable town. During the festival, local residents, including elementary and middle school students, dress as ancient people to perform rituals and parades. The event concludes with the ancient people delivering messages to modern attendees before "ascending to the heavens."<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals and Events|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Travel Back in Time to Ancient Japan, Featuring AKB's Nagao-san: Shibayama Haniwa Festival|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=Chiba Nippo Online|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Chiba Nippo Online|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}}, {{ill|Yamada-Houma Kofun Cluster|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群}}, and {{ill|Takada No. 2 Kofun|ja|高田二号墳}} * {{ill|Shibayama Haniwa Road|ja|芝山はにわ道}} * {{ill|Motohide Murayama|ja|村山元英}} * {{ill|Shuji Doi|ja|土井脩司}}

'''Shibayama Haniwa Festival''' (芝山はにわ祭) is a festival held in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|lt=Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}.
== Overview == The Shibayama Haniwa Festival was initially conceived by local volunteers as a way to unite residents divided by the {{ill|Narita Airport dispute|ja|成田空港問題}}.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Curator in My Town: Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum, Over 500 Kofun Once Existed / Chiba|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170807/ddl/k12/040/136000c|website=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-03-09|date=2017-08-07}}</ref> Officially described as a festival "born to reflect on and recall the wonders of ancient times,"<ref>{{Cite web|title=The 35th Shibayama Haniwa Festival is being held!|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref> it was first held on October 31, 1982 (Showa 57)<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2489/2015ShibayamaYouran_history_p8-15.pdf|title=Sixty Years of Shibayama History|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town|format=PDF}}</ref> and contributed significantly to fostering harmony among local residents.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Photo Album: The Showa Era in Sanbu|year=2012|publisher=Iki Publishing|page=130}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title="Slip Back in Time in the 'Land of Ancient Tombs': Shibayama Town"|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=Nikkei Electronic Edition|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>
Today, it is positioned as a community revitalization event, held annually on the second Sunday of November.<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Haniwa Festival Main Page|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=Haniwa Museum Website|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum}}</ref>
The festival draws inspiration from Shibayama's iconic {{ill|ancient burial mounds|ja|古墳群}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. The central concept involves interaction with "ancient people" who have descended into modern times to make a promise with the people of Shibayama to create a kind and livable town. During the festival, local residents, including elementary and middle school students, dress as ancient people to perform rituals and parades. The event concludes with the ancient people delivering messages to modern attendees before "ascending to the heavens."<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals and Events|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Travel Back in Time to Ancient Japan, Featuring AKB's Nagao-san: Shibayama Haniwa Festival|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=Chiba Nippo Online|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Chiba Nippo Online|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|芝山古墳群}}" to "|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}}"
revision.text:
'''Shibayama Haniwa Festival''' (芝山はにわ祭) is a festival held in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|lt=Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}.
== Overview == The Shibayama Haniwa Festival was initially conceived by local volunteers as a way to unite residents divided by the {{ill|Narita Airport dispute|ja|成田空港問題}}.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Curator in My Town: Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum, Over 500 Kofun Once Existed / Chiba|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170807/ddl/k12/040/136000c|website=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-03-09|date=2017-08-07}}</ref> Officially described as a festival "born to reflect on and recall the wonders of ancient times,"<ref>{{Cite web|title=The 35th Shibayama Haniwa Festival is being held!|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref> it was first held on October 31, 1982 (Showa 57)<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2489/2015ShibayamaYouran_history_p8-15.pdf|title=Sixty Years of Shibayama History|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town|format=PDF}}</ref> and contributed significantly to fostering harmony among local residents.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Photo Album: The Showa Era in Sanbu|year=2012|publisher=Iki Publishing|page=130}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title="Slip Back in Time in the 'Land of Ancient Tombs': Shibayama Town"|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=Nikkei Electronic Edition|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>
Today, it is positioned as a community revitalization event, held annually on the second Sunday of November.<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Haniwa Festival Main Page|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=Haniwa Museum Website|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum}}</ref>
The festival draws inspiration from Shibayama's iconic {{ill|ancient Kofuns|ja|古墳群}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. The central concept involves interaction with "ancient people" who have descended into modern times to make a promise with the people of Shibayama to create a kind and livable town. During the festival, local residents, including elementary and middle school students, dress as ancient people to perform rituals and parades. The event concludes with the ancient people delivering messages to modern attendees before "ascending to the heavens."<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals and Events|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Travel Back in Time to Ancient Japan, Featuring AKB's Nagao-san: Shibayama Haniwa Festival|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=Chiba Nippo Online|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Chiba Nippo Online|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}}, {{ill|Yamada-Houma Kofun Cluster|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群}}, and {{ill|Takada No. 2 Kofun|ja|高田二号墳}} * {{ill|Shibayama Haniwa Road|ja|芝山はにわ道}} * {{ill|Motohide Murayama|ja|村山元英}} * {{ill|Shuji Doi|ja|土井脩司}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/0000004151.html Shibayama Town Official Website - Tourism & Events - Shibayama Haniwa Festival] * [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1050001338458012928 Research Paper: "Festival and Regional Identity: Case Study Analysis of the Shibayama Haniwa Festival"]

The event concludes with the ancient people delivering messages to modern attendees before "ascending to the heavens."<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals and Events|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Travel Back in Time to Ancient Japan, Featuring AKB's Nagao-san: Shibayama Haniwa Festival|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=Chiba Nippo Online|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Chiba Nippo Online|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>

'''Shibayama Haniwa Festival''' (芝山はにわ祭) is a festival held in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|lt=Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}.
== Overview == The Shibayama Haniwa Festival was initially conceived by local volunteers as a way to unite residents divided by the {{ill|Narita Airport dispute|ja|成田空港問題}}.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Curator in My Town: Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum, Over 500 Kofun Once Existed / Chiba|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170807/ddl/k12/040/136000c|website=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-03-09|date=2017-08-07}}</ref> Officially described as a festival "born to reflect on and recall the wonders of ancient times,"<ref>{{Cite web|title=The 35th Shibayama Haniwa Festival is being held!|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref> it was first held on October 31, 1982 (Showa 57)<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2489/2015ShibayamaYouran_history_p8-15.pdf|title=Sixty Years of Shibayama History|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town|format=PDF}}</ref> and contributed significantly to fostering harmony among local residents.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Photo Album: The Showa Era in Sanbu|year=2012|publisher=Iki Publishing|page=130}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title="Slip Back in Time in the 'Land of Ancient Tombs': Shibayama Town"|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=Nikkei Electronic Edition|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>
Today, it is positioned as a community revitalization event, held annually on the second Sunday of November.<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Haniwa Festival Main Page|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=Haniwa Museum Website|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum}}</ref>
The festival draws inspiration from Shibayama's iconic {{ill|ancient Kofuns|ja|古墳群}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. The central concept involves interaction with "ancient people" who have descended into modern times to make a promise with the people of Shibayama to create a kind and livable town. During the festival, local residents, including elementary and middle school students, dress as ancient people to perform rituals and parades. The event concludes with the ancient people delivering messages to modern attendees before "ascending to the heavens."<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals and Events|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Travel Back in Time to Ancient Japan, Featuring AKB's Nagao-san: Shibayama Haniwa Festival|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=Chiba Nippo Online|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Chiba Nippo Online|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>

'''Shibayama Haniwa Festival''' (芝山はにわ祭) is a festival held in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|lt=Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}.
== Overview == The Shibayama Haniwa Festival was initially conceived by local volunteers as a way to unite residents divided by the {{ill|Narita Airport dispute|ja|成田空港問題}}.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Curator in My Town: Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum, Over 500 Kofun Once Existed / Chiba|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170807/ddl/k12/040/136000c|website=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-03-09|date=2017-08-07}}</ref> Officially described as a festival "born to reflect on and recall the wonders of ancient times,"<ref>{{Cite web|title=The 35th Shibayama Haniwa Festival is being held!|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref> it was first held on October 31, 1982 (Showa 57)<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2489/2015ShibayamaYouran_history_p8-15.pdf|title=Sixty Years of Shibayama History|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town|format=PDF}}</ref> and contributed significantly to fostering harmony among local residents.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Photo Album: The Showa Era in Sanbu|year=2012|publisher=Iki Publishing|page=130}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title="Slip Back in Time in the 'Land of Ancient Tombs': Shibayama Town"|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=Nikkei Electronic Edition|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>
Today, it is positioned as a community revitalization event, held annually on the second Sunday of November.<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Haniwa Festival Main Page|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=Haniwa Museum Website|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum}}</ref>
The festival draws inspiration from Shibayama's iconic {{ill|ancient Kofuns|ja|古墳群}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. The central concept involves interaction with "ancient people" who have descended into modern times to make a promise with the people of Shibayama to create a kind and livable town. During the festival, local residents, including elementary and middle school students, dress as ancient people to perform rituals and parades. The event concludes with the ancient people delivering messages to modern attendees before "ascending to the heavens."<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals and Events|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Travel Back in Time to Ancient Japan, Featuring AKB's Nagao-san: Shibayama Haniwa Festival|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=Chiba Nippo Online|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Chiba Nippo Online|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}}, {{ill|Yamada-Houma Kofun Cluster|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群}}, and {{ill|Takada No. 2 Kofun|ja|高田二号墳}} * {{ill|Shibayama Haniwa Road|ja|芝山はにわ道}} * {{ill|Motohide Murayama|ja|村山元英}} * {{ill|Shuji Doi|ja|土井脩司}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/0000004151.html Shibayama Town Official Website - Tourism & Events - Shibayama Haniwa Festival] * [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1050001338458012928 Research Paper: "Festival and Regional Identity: Case Study Analysis of the Shibayama Haniwa Festival"]

'''Shibayama Haniwa Festival''' (芝山はにわ祭) is a festival held in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|lt=Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}.
== Overview == The Shibayama Haniwa Festival was initially conceived by local volunteers as a way to unite residents divided by the {{ill|Narita Airport dispute|ja|成田空港問題}}.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Curator in My Town: Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum, Over 500 Kofun Once Existed / Chiba|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170807/ddl/k12/040/136000c|website=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-03-09|date=2017-08-07}}</ref> Officially described as a festival "born to reflect on and recall the wonders of ancient times,"<ref>{{Cite web|title=The 35th Shibayama Haniwa Festival is being held!|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref> it was first held on October 31, 1982 (Showa 57)<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2489/2015ShibayamaYouran_history_p8-15.pdf|title=Sixty Years of Shibayama History|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town|format=PDF}}</ref> and contributed significantly to fostering harmony among local residents.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Photo Album: The Showa Era in Sanbu|year=2012|publisher=Iki Publishing|page=130}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title="Slip Back in Time in the 'Land of Ancient Tombs': Shibayama Town"|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=Nikkei Electronic Edition|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>
Today, it is positioned as a community revitalization event, held annually on the second Sunday of November.<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Haniwa Festival Main Page|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=Haniwa Museum Website|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum}}</ref>
The festival draws inspiration from Shibayama's iconic {{ill|ancient Kofuns|ja|古墳群}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. The central concept involves interaction with "ancient people" who have descended into modern times to make a promise with the people of Shibayama to create a kind and livable town. During the festival, local residents, including elementary and middle school students, dress as ancient people to perform rituals and parades. The event concludes with the ancient people delivering messages to modern attendees before "ascending to the heavens."<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals and Events|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Travel Back in Time to Ancient Japan, Featuring AKB's Nagao-san: Shibayama Haniwa Festival|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=Chiba Nippo Online|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Chiba Nippo Online|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}}, {{ill|Yamada-Houma Kofun Cluster|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群}}, and {{ill|Takada No. 2 Kofun|ja|高田二号墳}} * {{ill|Shibayama Haniwa Road|ja|芝山はにわ道}} * {{ill|Motohide Murayama|ja|村山元英}} * {{ill|Shuji Doi|ja|土井脩司}}

'''Shibayama Haniwa Festival''' (芝山はにわ祭) is a festival held in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|lt=Shibayama|ja|芝山町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}.
== Overview == The Shibayama Haniwa Festival was initially conceived by local volunteers as a way to unite residents divided by the {{ill|Narita Airport dispute|ja|成田空港問題}}.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Curator in My Town: Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum, Over 500 Kofun Once Existed / Chiba|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170807/ddl/k12/040/136000c|website=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-03-09|date=2017-08-07}}</ref> Officially described as a festival "born to reflect on and recall the wonders of ancient times,"<ref>{{Cite web|title=The 35th Shibayama Haniwa Festival is being held!|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref> it was first held on October 31, 1982 (Showa 57)<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2489/2015ShibayamaYouran_history_p8-15.pdf|title=Sixty Years of Shibayama History|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town|format=PDF}}</ref> and contributed significantly to fostering harmony among local residents.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Photo Album: The Showa Era in Sanbu|year=2012|publisher=Iki Publishing|page=130}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title="Slip Back in Time in the 'Land of Ancient Tombs': Shibayama Town"|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=Nikkei Electronic Edition|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>
Today, it is positioned as a community revitalization event, held annually on the second Sunday of November.<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Haniwa Festival Main Page|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=Haniwa Museum Website|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum}}</ref>
The festival draws inspiration from Shibayama's iconic {{ill|ancient Kofuns|ja|古墳群}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. The central concept involves interaction with "ancient people" who have descended into modern times to make a promise with the people of Shibayama to create a kind and livable town. During the festival, local residents, including elementary and middle school students, dress as ancient people to perform rituals and parades. The event concludes with the ancient people delivering messages to modern attendees before "ascending to the heavens."<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals and Events|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Travel Back in Time to Ancient Japan, Featuring AKB's Nagao-san: Shibayama Haniwa Festival|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=Chiba Nippo Online|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Chiba Nippo Online|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>

== Overview == The Shibayama Haniwa Festival was initially conceived by local volunteers as a way to unite residents divided by the {{ill|Narita Airport dispute|ja|成田空港問題|12=draft}}.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Curator in My Town: Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum, Over 500 Kofun Once Existed / Chiba|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170807/ddl/k12/040/136000c|website=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-03-09|date=2017-08-07}}</ref> Officially described as a festival "born to reflect on and recall the wonders of ancient times,"<ref>{{Cite web|title=The 35th Shibayama Haniwa Festival is being held!|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref> it was first held on October 31, 1982 (Showa 57)<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2489/2015ShibayamaYouran_history_p8-15.pdf|title=Sixty Years of Shibayama History|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town|format=PDF}}</ref> and contributed significantly to fostering harmony among local residents.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Photo Album: The Showa Era in Sanbu|year=2012|publisher=Iki Publishing|page=130}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title="Slip Back in Time in the 'Land of Ancient Tombs': Shibayama Town"|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=Nikkei Electronic Edition|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>
Today, it is positioned as a community revitalization event, held annually on the second Sunday of November.<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Haniwa Festival Main Page|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=Haniwa Museum Website|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum}}</ref>
The festival draws inspiration from Shibayama's iconic {{ill|ancient Kofuns|ja|古墳群|12=draft}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}. The central concept involves interaction with "ancient people" who have descended into modern times to make a promise with the people of Shibayama to create a kind and livable town. During the festival, local residents, including elementary and middle school students, dress as ancient people to perform rituals and parades. The event concludes with the ancient people delivering messages to modern attendees before "ascending to the heavens."<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals and Events|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Travel Back in Time to Ancient Japan, Featuring AKB's Nagao-san: Shibayama Haniwa Festival|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=Chiba Nippo Online|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Chiba Nippo Online|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>

'''Shibayama Haniwa Festival''' (芝山はにわ祭) is a festival held in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|lt=Shibayama|ja|芝山町|12=draft}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == The Shibayama Haniwa Festival was initially conceived by local volunteers as a way to unite residents divided by the {{ill|Narita Airport dispute|ja|成田空港問題|12=draft}}.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Curator in My Town: Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum, Over 500 Kofun Once Existed / Chiba|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170807/ddl/k12/040/136000c|website=Mainichi Shimbun|accessdate=2019-03-09|date=2017-08-07}}</ref> Officially described as a festival "born to reflect on and recall the wonders of ancient times,"<ref>{{Cite web|title=The 35th Shibayama Haniwa Festival is being held!|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/info/614/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref> it was first held on October 31, 1982 (Showa 57)<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2489/2015ShibayamaYouran_history_p8-15.pdf|title=Sixty Years of Shibayama History|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town|format=PDF}}</ref> and contributed significantly to fostering harmony among local residents.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Photo Album: The Showa Era in Sanbu|year=2012|publisher=Iki Publishing|page=130}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title="Slip Back in Time in the 'Land of Ancient Tombs': Shibayama Town"|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO24736060W7A211C1CC0000/|website=Nikkei Electronic Edition|accessdate=2019-03-09}}</ref>
Today, it is positioned as a community revitalization event, held annually on the second Sunday of November.<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Haniwa Festival Main Page|url=http://www.haniwakan.com/matsuri/top.html|website=Haniwa Museum Website|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum}}</ref>
The festival draws inspiration from Shibayama's iconic {{ill|ancient Kofuns|ja|古墳群|12=draft}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}. The central concept involves interaction with "ancient people" who have descended into modern times to make a promise with the people of Shibayama to create a kind and livable town. During the festival, local residents, including elementary and middle school students, dress as ancient people to perform rituals and parades. The event concludes with the ancient people delivering messages to modern attendees before "ascending to the heavens."<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals and Events|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Travel Back in Time to Ancient Japan, Featuring AKB's Nagao-san: Shibayama Haniwa Festival|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=Chiba Nippo Online|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Chiba Nippo Online|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamada-Houma Kofun Cluster|ja|山田・宝馬古墳群|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Takada No. 2 Kofun|ja|高田二号墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shibayama Haniwa Road|ja|芝山はにわ道|12=draft}} * {{ill|Motohide Murayama|ja|村山元英|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shuji Doi|ja|土井脩司|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/0000004151.html Shibayama Town Official Website - Tourism & Events - Shibayama Haniwa Festival] * [https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1050001338458012928 Research Paper: "Festival and Regional Identity: Case Study Analysis of the Shibayama Haniwa Festival"]

The event concludes with the ancient people delivering messages to modern attendees before "ascending to the heavens."<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Festivals and Events|url=http://shibayama-kankou.com/event/|website=Shibayama Town Tourism Portal|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Shibayama Town Tourism Association}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Travel Back in Time to Ancient Japan, Featuring AKB's Nagao-san: Shibayama Haniwa Festival|url=https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/local/224238|website=Chiba Nippo Online|accessdate=2019-03-09|publisher=Chiba Nippo Online|date=2014-11-10}}</ref>
